title: Misatos escape pod
wikitext:
[]: Misato's escape pod?             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Fri May 04, 2012 7:06 pm I am sure someone has a quality answer on this somewhere, but being new to the whole eva thing I understand all the points, but I have one lingering question...Misato at Antartica and her escape. Was it a pod they just kinda had there, or could it of been an early entry plug that had to do with the contact experiment or the future of it. Besides the shape the plug idea stick with me because they were restricting Adam, and it wouldn't seem illogical to keep pushing boundaries in such a way. I am not trying to say Misato was a pilot or anything else like that, just that they might have been pushing that level of mental contact. As a follow up, do we know how much science knew about LCL or anything else that would help the sync of a pilot at that time?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri May 04, 2012 7:56 pm The object Misato was put in is an "escape capsule", and it was situated in the facility on the surface. There's no implied link between them and the contact experiment, which was taking place deep in the White Moon. Though, there are any number of questions one could smack those escape pods with. No idea if LCL had been discovered by that point. The fluid doesn't seem to be exclusive to Lilith's bloodstream; Eva cores seem to channel it, so Adam's core might, as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Sat May 05, 2012 6:40 pm Honestly, I don't think they'd have thought that far ahead. They just discovered a gigantic alien humanoid on their planet, so it's not likely that they'd have thought of a means to control it. Even if they wanted to, it's less likely that sufficient technology would be available for them to control it.

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Sun May 06, 2012 4:18 pm I'm sorry I'm slipping and have had no sleep in 2 days, but do they actually say they discovered it in 2000. I feel like they do I just can't recall it.

[]:             Quote Post by Henry Spencer » Sun May 06, 2012 5:57 pm They discovered it in 2000, yes, but that is also when the Second Impact occurred. It's likely that the expedition didn't last long and ended in disaster. Why am I suddenly reminded of At The Mountains Of Madness ? On an incidental note: don't worry. Sleep deprivation happens to all of us.
